fr:mainmatter.html:p: The later modality on presheaves is given at each object by mapping out of the strict downset: {\mathopen {}\left (\rhd P\right )\mathclose {}}\,{\mathopen {}\left (x\right )\mathclose {}} = \mathsf {Psh}\,\mathcal {C}{\mathopen {}\left (\mathsf {StrictDown}{\mathopen {}\left (x\right )\mathclose {}},\, P\right )\mathclose {}}. An element of \rhd P at x is a coherent choice of P-elements for all objects strictly smaller than x.

Restriction in \rhd P along f : y \to x precomposes with the induced map \mathsf {StrictDown}{\mathopen {}\left (y\right )\mathclose {}} \to \mathsf {StrictDown}{\mathopen {}\left (x\right )\mathclose {}}. At an object of minimal degree the strict downset is empty, so \rhd P is trivial there.

Via functoriality, every presheaf restricts to smaller indices: the map \mathsf {next} : P \to \rhd P sends an element p over x to the family of all its restrictions along morphisms from strictly lower objects.

fr:title.text: Code locations for the guarded recursion implementation

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Code locations for the guarded recursion implementation

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Where each note lives in cubical-categorical-logic. Module names are relative to the repository root.

html:ul.html:li: Algebras as a displayed category, Initial algebra — Cubical.Categories.Displayed.Instances.Algebras
Coalgebras as a displayed category, Terminal coalgebra — Cubical.Categories.Displayed.Instances.Coalgebras
The comparison functor, Monadicity and comonadicity — Cubical.Categories.Displayed.Instances.EilenbergMoore.Comparison, …CoEilenbergMoore.Comparison
The adjoint triple, Presheaves are monadic and comonadic over families — Cubical.Categories.Presheaf.Family.Base
Well-founded posets are thin direct categories — Cubical.Categories.Direct.Instances.Poset, …Instances.Nat
Sieves, Proper and maximal sieves — Cubical.Categories.Presheaf.Sieve, Cubical.Categories.Subobject.Base
The strict downset, its maximality, Later on presheaves, Löb induction for presheaves, Later on families, Löb induction on families — Cubical.Categories.Direct.StrictDownset
Locally contractive endofunctors, Local contractivity makes every coalgebra recursive — Cubical.Categories.Direct.LocallyContractive
Hylomorphisms, Recursive coalgebras, The coalgebra-to-algebra profunctor, Unique fixed points — Cubical.Categories.Displayed.Instances.Algebras.Recursive
Selective ordered semirings — Cubical.Algebra.OrderedSemiring and its Selective, MinPlus, Bool submodules

fr:mainmatter.html:p: A predecessor of x is a top element of its strict downset: a strict morphism \rho : p \to x through which every strict morphism into x factors uniquely. Equivalently, \mathsf {StrictDown}{\mathopen {}\left (x\right )\mathclose {}} \cong \mathsf {y}\, p — the downset is representable.

The Yoneda lemma then collapses later to evaluation: \rhd P\,{\mathopen {}\left (x\right )\mathclose {}} = \mathsf {Psh}\,\mathcal {C}{\mathopen {}\left (\mathsf {StrictDown}{\mathopen {}\left (x\right )\mathclose {}},\, P\right )\mathclose {}} \cong \mathsf {Psh}\,\mathcal {C}{\mathopen {}\left (\mathsf {y}\, p,\, P\right )\mathclose {}} \cong P{\mathopen {}\left (p\right )\mathclose {}}, with \mathsf {next} becoming restriction along \rho . The name is from the naturals: every strict map into n+1 factors through n \to n+1, so on \omega — the topos of trees — later is just the shift \rhd P\,{\mathopen {}\left (0\right )\mathclose {}} \cong \top , \qquad \rhd P\,{\mathopen {}\left (n+1\right )\mathclose {}} \cong P{\mathopen {}\left (n\right )\mathclose {}}, and a Löb step is a base value together with a rule producing the value at n+1 from the value at n.
When this predecessor exists, we can give a simpler description of later, as in the topos of trees, but this may not be possible in all direct categories.

fr:title.text: Löb induction on the walking cospan

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Löb induction on the walking cospan

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The walking cospan L \to M \leftarrow R is a thin direct category — \mathrm {deg} L = \mathrm {deg} R = 0 and \mathrm {deg} M = 1 — and the smallest one whose past branches. Presheaves on it are spans of sets.
Later at the apex joins the two legs, \rhd P\,{\mathopen {}\left (M\right )\mathclose {}} \cong P{\mathopen {}\left (L\right )\mathclose {}} \times P{\mathopen {}\left (R\right )\mathclose {}}

fr:title.text: Löb induction on the walking parallel pair

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Löb induction on the walking parallel pair

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The walking parallel pair — objects V, E with two parallel morphisms s, t : V \to E — is a direct category with \mathrm {deg} V = 0 and \mathrm {deg} E = 1, the smallest one that is not thin. Presheaves on it are directed multigraphs.

Later computes to \rhd P\,{\mathopen {}\left (V\right )\mathclose {}} \cong \top , \qquad \rhd P\,{\mathopen {}\left (E\right )\mathclose {}} \cong P{\mathopen {}\left (V\right )\mathclose {}} \times P{\mathopen {}\left (V\right )\mathclose {}}, one factor per parallel morphism — a plurality no poset can produce — and \mathsf {next} sends an edge to its pair of endpoints: it is the boundary map.
By naturality, a Löb step \rhd P \to P is a choice of vertex together with a self-loop at it, and its fixed point is that self-loop.

A grammar assigns to each string w a set B\,{\mathopen {}\left (w\right )\mathclose {}} of parses — a family over strings. Strings with the proper-suffix ordering give a well-founded poset, and thus a direct category. Semantically, the later modality is expressed as {\mathopen {}\left (\rhd B\right )\mathclose {}}\,{\mathopen {}\left (w\right )\mathclose {}} = \prod _{u < w} B\,{\mathopen {}\left (u\right )\mathclose {}}, a product over the proper suffixes of w. An element of \rhd B at w is a B-parse for every strictly shorter remainder of the input: "B, after consuming at least one character". Internal to Dependent Lambek Calculus, we may represent later as \rhd B = \mathchoice {\textstyle \And _{{\mathopen {}\left (w \in \texttt {String}^{+}\right )\mathclose {}}}}{\textstyle \And _{{\mathopen {}\left (w \in \texttt {String}^{+}\right )\mathclose {}}}}{\scriptstyle \And _{{\mathopen {}\left (w \in \texttt {String}^{+}\right )\mathclose {}}}}{\scriptscriptstyle \And _{{\mathopen {}\left (w \in \texttt {String}^{+}\right )\mathclose {}}}} \sqrt [w]{B}

Write X \Rightarrow Y for the presheaf of morphisms X \to Y. An endofunctor F on presheaves is locally contractive when its action on morphisms factors through later: there is a map F_\delta : \rhd {\mathopen {}\left (X \Rightarrow Y\right )\mathclose {}} \to {\mathopen {}\left (F X \Rightarrow F Y\right )\mathclose {}}

Later takes a limit over smaller indices. Dually, the earlier modality takes a colimit over larger indices: an element of \lhd P at x is a P-element sitting at some object y strictly above x, carried down along a chosen morphism x \to y.
Earlier is left adjoint to later: \lhd \dashv \rhd
Under this adjunction, \mathsf {next} : P \to \rhd P corresponds to \mathsf {prev} : \lhd P \to P,.

fr:title.text: The strict downset sieve of a direct category

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The strict downset sieve of a direct category

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Let \mathcal {C} carry a direct structure. The strict downset \mathsf {StrictDown}{\mathopen {}\left (x\right )\mathclose {}} of an object x is the presheaf of morphisms into x from strictly lower objects: \mathsf {StrictDown}{\mathopen {}\left (x\right )\mathclose {}}\,{\mathopen {}\left (y\right )\mathclose {}} = {\mathopen {}\left \{f : y \to x \mid y \prec x\right \}\mathclose {}}, with restriction by precomposition — well defined since degrees are non-decreasing, so precomposing can only stay strictly below.

The evident inclusion \mathsf {StrictDown}{\mathopen {}\left (x\right )\mathclose {}} \rightarrowtail よ\, x makes \mathsf {StrictDown}{\mathopen {}\left (x\right )\mathclose {}} a sieve on x. It is moreover a proper sieve, as it exlcudes the identity.
